IOBR 0.99.99
  • Reference
    • Multidimensional Decoding Tumor Microenvironment for Immuno-Oncology Research

Reference profiles for B cell–related deconvolution (EPIC/IOBR)

Source: R/data_doc.R
BRef.Rd

BRef provides the B-cell–related reference gene expression profiles used by the EPIC deconvolution model. Within the IOBR workflow, this dataset is used to estimate immune cell fractions in bulk RNA-seq samples. The dataset contains normalized gene expression profiles, expression variability, and the signature genes required by EPIC.

Usage

data(BRef)

Format

A list of length 3:

  • refProfiles: double matrix, approximately 49902 × 6

  • refProfiles.var: double matrix, approximately 49902 × 6

  • sigGenes: character vector, approximately 65 genes

Details

This reference dataset includes the following components:

refProfiles

A numeric matrix of size nGenes × nCellTypes. Rows correspond to genes, and columns correspond to B-cell–related subtypes. The values generally represent normalized expression (e.g., TPM). EPIC uses this matrix as the baseline expression profile for the reference cell types during deconvolution.

refProfiles.var

A numeric matrix with identical dimensions as refProfiles. It represents gene-level expression variability for each cell type. These variability estimates are used as weights in EPIC’s weighted least-squares optimization. If variability is not used in a given workflow, EPIC assumes the same variability for all genes.

sigGenes

A character vector listing the signature genes used by EPIC for B-cell deconvolution. Only these genes are included in the fitting procedure to improve the robustness and biological specificity of the model.

On this page

Developed by Dongqiang Zeng, Yiran Fang, Shixiang Wang, Qingcong Luo, Hongqian Qian.
